chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we can also create numpy arrays from scratch

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: to create numpy arrays from scratch we have different interesting functions

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: for example we have a function np.zeros

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: in this function we can pass our dimensions

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: for example i want a 2x3 size 2 rows 3 columns 2d array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: which we want to prefill with 0 values

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so we can create an array like this with np.zeros

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we can print array 1 and also print the shape of array 1

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: this will actually be without an e

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so we run it and we get numpy array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: 2d array of 2x3 and these are the dimensions

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: if we want we can change the type of the array we are creating from scratch

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: there are two ways to change the type

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: first of all when we are creating it from scratch

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we can send dtype is equal to some value

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: by default it is creating zeros in floating values

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we want integer 64 to be created

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: now this integer 64 or integer 120 or integer 30

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: it actually depends on our system

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: there are many windows systems that allow integer 64 or integer 32

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so depending upon your system you can either create in 64 or in 32

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we will rerun it

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: this will actually be in double quotes

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: if we rerun it then we have integer values in numpy array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: along with this we can also create a numpy array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and fill it with ones

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so for ones we can have ones here

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: let's give it the name array2

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and we will prefill it with ones

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: let's print these

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so this is our ones array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and we can give it different shapes

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: if we want to create a one dimensional array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: then we can simply give it dimensions like this

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and we have 1D array of ones of size 5

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we also have another function which is called full

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: in full function we prefill with a particular value

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: like here if we have full this can be array 3

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so what we do is first we will send our dimensions

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: let's suppose our dimension we want it to be 3 x 4

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and after that we send the numbers with which we want to prefill our array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so let's suppose we want to add 100 values on all the cells

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so let's create this array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so our array is created and all the values are equal to 100

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: along with this we also have another function which is called i

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: numpy.ifunction

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: what happens with ifunction is that

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: it creates an identity matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: what exactly is an identity matrix?

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: this function is used for identity matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and inside this we have to pass a single value

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: this value can be anything

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: it could be 3, 4, 5, any number

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: now what is the meaning of identity matrix?

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: identity matrix is a square matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: let's suppose we have already created a square matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: what is this?

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: this matrix is 3 x 3

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: whenever we have a matrix in our array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we have to pass a single value

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so let's say we have a matrix in our array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and we have a square matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: whenever row and column are same in a matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we call it a square matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so identity matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we represent it with i

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: in math

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: is a square matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: which has only diagonal values 1

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: diagonal means

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: this left diagonal value is 1

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and all the other values are 0

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so if this was an identity matrix of size 3

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so what are the elements inside this?

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: all these values are 1

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and all the other values are 0

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so this becomes an identity matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: now the thing to think about is

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: why are we discussing this special matrix?

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: because identity matrix is used heavily in math

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: to perform a lot of operations on vectors

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: that's why identity matrix has its importance

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: which we will see in many algorithms

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: let's create it

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: with this function

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we can also print it

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so we have created an identity matrix

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: of size 3 x 3

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: plus we also have another function

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: which is called a range function

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: a range function works like

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: the range of python

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: python range means

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: range function has 3 parameters

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: first parameter is start

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: second is end

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and third is step

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so we pass 3 parameters like this

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: let's suppose we want to create an array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: of 1 to 10

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so it will be created like this

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so we will have an array of values

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: from 1 to 9

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: in which 10 is going to be there

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: in which 10 is going to be non-inclusive

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: here if we do 11

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so we will have values from 1 to 10

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: if we want we can also add a step

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: for example if we want to skip every second element

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so in that case

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we will only have an array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: with odd values

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we can also start it from 0

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: in that case we will have all even values

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so in this way we can create an array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: of even values

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: next function which we have

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: that is called lin space

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: what we do with lin space

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we create evenly spaced

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: arrays

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: this was actually

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: range elements

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so we create evenly spaced arrays

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: from lin space

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: what does it mean

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: let's suppose in range of 0 to 100

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we want to create an array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: but i only want the

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: array size to be 4

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: i want the size of the array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: to be equal to 4

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so in that case

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: we start it from 1

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so in our array we should have only 4 elements

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: but the value of those 4 elements

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: should lie in range of 1 to 100

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so we can use lin space

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: i can also print the values

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so our array will be created

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: in this way

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: here we have defined starting value

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and ending value

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: and we have defined total numbers

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: if we do 3 here

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so we will have an array of 3 elements

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: if 1 is start and 100 is end

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: then we will have 50 value in middle

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so this is exactly how we create

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: an evenly spaced array

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: so these are different functions

chunks.title: Creating
chunks.text: which we use to create numpy arrays

this function is used for identity matrix and inside this we have to pass a single value this value can be anything it could be 3, 4, 5, any number now what is the meaning of identity matrix? identity matrix is a square matrix let's suppose we have already created a square matrix what is this? this matrix is 3 x 3 whenever we have a matrix in our array we have to pass a single value so let's say we have a matrix in our array and we have a square matrix whenever row and column are same in a matrix we call it a square matrix so identity matrix we represent it with i in math is a square matrix which has only diagonal values 1 diagonal means this left diagonal value is 1 and all the other values are 0 so if this was an identity matrix of size 3 so what are the elements inside this? all these values are 1 and all the other values are 0 so this becomes an identity matrix now the thing to think about is why are we discussing this special matrix? because identity matrix is used heavily in created an identity matrix of size 3 x 3 plus we also have another function which is called a range function a range function works like the range of python python range means range function has 3 parameters first parameter is start second is end and third is step so we pass 3 parameters like this let's suppose we want to create an array of 1 to 10 so it will be created like this so we will have an array of values from 1 to 9 in which 10 is going to be there in which 10 is going to be non-inclusive here if we do 11 so we will have values from 1 to 10 if we want we can also add a step for example if we want to skip every second element so in that case we will only have an array with odd values we can also start it from 0 in that case we will have all even values so in this way we can create an array of even values next function which we have that is called lin space what we do with lin space we create evenly spaced arrays this was actually range elements so we create evenly spaced arrays from lin space what does it mean let's suppose in range of 0 to 100 we want to create an array but i only want the array size to be 4 i want the size of the array to be equal to 4 so in that case we start it from 1 so in our array we should have only 4 elements but the value of those 4 elements should lie in range of 1 to 100 so we can use lin space i can also print the values so our end then we will have 50 value in middle so this is exactly how we create an evenly spaced array so these are different functions which we use to create numpy arrays
